title: Randia ngoyensis
taxonomy: Randia ngoyensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. The plant is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is a member of the genus Randia, which comprises several species of flowering plants. The plant's classification is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, and the phylum Magnoliophyta. The family Rubiaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as coffee or madder family.

intro: Randia ngoyensis, also known as the African Randia, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its woody stem and leaves with elliptical shapes. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include the treatment of various health issues such as fever, malaria, and respiratory infections, thanks to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Randia ngoyensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, including randicine and randialdehyde, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Randia ngoyensis is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or ointment to treat various ailments, and is also used as an ingredient in some modern herbal remedies.
